Hi Ben,

Thank you for contacting Canadian Fitness Repair regarding your Horizon
Fitness treadmill issue.

Based on the symptoms you described, this type of problem can often be
resolved with a proper tune-up and maintenance service, however we can only
confirm the exact cause and solution after inspecting the treadmill in
person.

the following details:

Address: 35 Neptune Drive, St. Catharines, ON, L2M 2S4

The service fee is $150, which includes:

- On-site repair completed the same day if possible

Issue reported:
Treadmill works for a short time, then the belt speeds up, slows down, and
stops abruptly. Console beeps and freezes, and the buttons become
unresponsive. This usually happens around the 10-minute mark. Belt has
already been lubricated multiple times.

After diagnosis, we will advise whether the treadmill can be repaired or if
replacement should be considered.

Please note:
The $150 covers labor and service only. Any required parts (if needed) are
not included and will be quoted separately before proceeding.

inspect and resolve the issue. Please reply to confirm if this time works

adjust.

Sam
Canadian Fitness Repair

Treadmill belt slipping. Hesitates when weight applied. Lubricated belt and
improved, but still hesitates too much.

Hi Milja,


Thank you for reaching out regarding your ProForm Carbon TL treadmill.


Based on your description, the belt slipping and hesitation when weight is
applied could indicate an issue with the drive motor, control board, or
belt tension. To determine the exact cause, we would need to perform an
on-site diagnostic.


Our diagnostic appointment fee is $160, which covers a full inspection of
your treadmill. If the issue can be repaired on the same day without
requiring parts, we will complete the repair at no additional labor cost.
Please note that parts are not included in the diagnostic fee.


We are available to come on Monday, January 26.


Please let us know if you’d like to schedule the appointment or if you have
any questions.


Best regards,

Canadian Fitness Repair

Hi Nael,

Thank you for contacting Canadian Fitness Repair.

We do service the Oakville area and can certainly help with preventative
maintenance and the issues you described, including belt slipping, speed
inconsistencies, and overheating concerns.

To provide accurate pricing and prepare properly for the service, could you
please email us the treadmill model number and serial number? This will
allow us to determine the correct walking belt price, if replacement is
required.

Additionally, when you mention overheating at speeds 5–6 MPH, could you
please confirm what exactly is overheating?
For example:
- Is it the walking belt (excessive friction or burning smell)?
- The motor area?
- Or does the treadmill shut down or display an error?

Once we have this information, we can advise on the next steps and confirm
service details.

We look forward to your reply.

Best regards,

Canadian Fitness Repair

Service Request Repairs & Tune-Ups Client Type Individual Equipment Type
Treadmill Equipment Manufacturer Horizon Fitness Model Number 7.0 AT Serial
Number Preferred Booking Date & Time 2025-10-28T17:00 Description of Issue Dear
Technician,

I have a Horizon 7.0 AT treadmill purchased at the end of 2019. It was stored
for about one year at my previous house, where it could not start properly.

After moving to my new place, the treadmill could finally start again, but when
it first powered on, I heard a small pop or burst sound from the motor area.
Since then, it can run, but the noise becomes extremely loud once the speed goes
above 8 mph.

When I run above 9.5 mph, the treadmill becomes very loud, and after about two
laps it always trips the circuit breaker. When the treadmill runs without anyone
on it, it can still reach up to 12 mph, though I can hear a high-pitched whine
and a whooshing sound.

I have already applied lubricant to the running belt, but the problem remains.
Based on these symptoms, I suspect something may be wrong with the motor,
capacitor, or bearings.

Could you please help check and confirm what repairs might be needed, and
provide an estimate for inspection and service?
The treadmill is located in Waterloo, Ontario.

Thank you very much for your help.

Best regards,
Rumiyah Chen

Hi Rumiyah,


Thank you for your detailed message.


We can schedule a diagnostic appointment on Monday, November 3rd between
11:30 AM and 1:00 PM.

The diagnostic fee is $150, which includes inspection of the motor, control
board, belt, bearings, and all electrical and mechanical components to
identify the root cause of the issue.


Payment can be made via e-transfer to canadianfitnessrepair@gmail.com
(please note GST on e-transfer) or cash at the time of service.


Please confirm if this date and time work for you so we can reserve the
appointment.


Thank you,

Canadian Fitness Repair
